title: nymphaea ampla
intro: Nymphaea ampla, also known as the Egyptian lotus, is a water lily species native to tropical Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating rheumatism, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of Nymphaea ampla include extracts, infusions, and decoctions, which can be used to support the immune system and promote overall well-being.
